claim: The registry already contains the intended shared mode source data, including skill id, workflowMode, runtimeLoopType, backendKind, packet, command, agent, artifactRoot, and aliases.
Advisor routing is currently hardcoded despite hub docs saying the registry is the source of truth.
The existing public contract is already {skill: deep-loop-workflows, mode: workflowMode}, so registry-driven mapping can preserve parity fixtures.
Model B would break the one-identity keystone because recursive graph metadata discovery would rank packet graph-metadata files as separate skills and parseSkillMetadata requires skill_id==folder.
Registry aliases are not yet complete enough to replace current advisor aliases without expansion or an advisorAliases/advisorCanonical field.
If TS reads the registry, advisor freshness and daemon watching must include mode-registry.json or registry edits can be invisible to cached recommendations.
